Name: Ethereum Consensus
Description: All EVM rollups and most DeFi depend on Ethereum's security budget and validator set
Provider/Bottleneck: Ethereum validators (~800K but concentrated in Lido/Coinbase/Kraken)
Failure Scenario: Validator cartel censorship; 51% attack; social layer capture - entire L2 ecosystem halts
Affected Primitives: Rollups, Settlement Layers, Data Availability Layers
Mitigation Strategies: Multi-chain deployment; sovereign rollups on Celestia; Bitcoin settlement alternatives

Name: Cloud Infrastructure (AWS/GCP/Azure)
Description: Majority of blockchain nodes run on 3 cloud providers; includes RPC endpoints and indexers
Failure Scenario: Coordinated deplatforming could take down 60%+ of network nodes within hours; happened to Parler
Mitigation Strategies: Bare metal node incentives; geographic distribution requirements; decentralized RPC (Pocket Network)

Name: Semiconductor Supply Chain
Description: All hardware wallets, ZK provers, and FHE accelerators depend on TSMC/Samsung fabrication
Failure Scenario: Taiwan conflict disrupts all advanced chip production; 2-3 year delay to rebuild capacity elsewhere
Affected Primitives: FHE, ZK Proofs, Key Management, Hardware Wallets
Mitigation Strategies: Stockpiling; less advanced node alternatives; geographic fab diversification (long-term)

Name: Stablecoin Banking
Description: USDC/USDT require correspondent banking; Circle's banking relationships are concentrated
Provider/Bottleneck: Circle (Silvergate collapsed, now BNY Mellon, Cross River)
Failure Scenario: Banking partner failure freezes redemptions; happened with SVB causing USDC depeg to $0.87
Mitigation Strategies: Decentralized stablecoins (DAI); multi-bank relationships; non-USD denominations

Name: Domain Name System
Description: All dApps with web frontends depend on ICANN-controlled DNS; ENS doesn't help if browser can't resolve
Failure Scenario: Domain seizure (happened to Tornado Cash); DNS hijacking redirects users to phishing
Mitigation Strategies: IPFS frontends; ENS + IPFS resolution; alternative DNS roots (Handshake)

Name: Oracle Data Sources
Description: Chainlink and Pyth ultimately source from centralized exchanges (Binance, Coinbase, etc)
Failure Scenario: Exchange manipulation or failure corrupts all dependent price feeds; cascading liquidations
Affected Primitives: Oracle Infrastructure, Lending/Borrowing, Perpetual DEXs, Insurance
Mitigation Strategies: Multi-source aggregation; TWAP mechanisms; circuit breakers; on-chain CLOB price discovery

Name: Trusted Setup Ceremonies
Description: Many ZK systems (Groth16, older PLONK) require trusted setup; compromise breaks soundness
Provider/Bottleneck: Ceremony participants; Zcash Powers of Tau had 87 participants
Failure Scenario: If all participants colluded or were compromised, fake proofs could be generated
Mitigation Strategies: STARKs (no trusted setup); Halo2 recursive proofs; transparent setup alternatives

Name: MEV Infrastructure
Description: Most Ethereum transactions route through Flashbots; block builders are highly concentrated
Provider/Bottleneck: Flashbots (~80% of blocks), handful of builders
Failure Scenario: Builder censorship (already happening - OFAC compliant builders); MEV extraction centralizes
Affected Primitives: AMMs, Intent-Based Systems, All DEX activity
Mitigation Strategies: Encrypted mempools; threshold encryption; inclusion lists (protocol level)

Name: Sequencer Centralization
Description: Every major L2 (Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, zkSync) runs a single centralized sequencer
Provider/Bottleneck: Offchain Labs, OP Labs, Coinbase, Matter Labs
Failure Scenario: Sequencer downtime halts L2 (happened multiple times); censorship at sequencer level
Mitigation Strategies: Decentralized sequencer roadmaps; based rollups; shared sequencing (Espresso)

Name: Auditor Concentration
Description: 3-4 firms audit most high-value DeFi; their capacity limits ecosystem growth
Provider/Bottleneck: Trail of Bits, OpenZeppelin, Spearbit, Consensys Diligence
Failure Scenario: Missed vulnerability in widely-used code propagates everywhere; auditor reputation attack
Mitigation Strategies: Formal verification; multiple audit requirement; bug bounties as complement

Name: Legal Entity Jurisdiction
Description: Most protocols have a legal entity somewhere; that jurisdiction can compel action
Failure Scenario: Regulatory action against foundation forces protocol changes; happened with Tornado Cash dev arrest
Affected Primitives: Governance, DAO Frameworks, All protocols with identifiable teams
Mitigation Strategies: Legal wrapper diversification; progressive decentralization; anon development

Name: Bridge Validator Sets
Description: Cross-chain bridges typically secured by small multisig or validator set
Failure Scenario: $2.8B already stolen; validator collusion or key compromise drains bridge
Mitigation Strategies: ZK light client proofs; optimistic bridges with fraud proofs; native rollup bridging

Name: RPC Provider Concentration
Description: Most wallets and dApps use Infura/Alchemy for blockchain access; users rarely run own nodes
Failure Scenario: Provider outage or censorship breaks user access; Infura blocked Venezuela
Mitigation Strategies: Light clients in browser; decentralized RPC (Pocket, Lava); wallet-embedded nodes

Name: Fiat On/Off Ramps
Description: Converting between crypto and fiat requires regulated exchanges or payment processors
Failure Scenario: Ramp closure strands users in crypto-only; Operation Choke Point 2.0 targeting crypto banking
Mitigation Strategies: P2P markets; stablecoin merchant adoption; regulatory arbitrage across jurisdictions

Name: GitHub/Code Hosting
Description: Nearly all crypto projects host code on Microsoft-owned GitHub; includes CI/CD pipelines
Failure Scenario: Repository takedown removes development coordination; happened to Tornado Cash
Mitigation Strategies: Self-hosted GitLab; Radicle (decentralized git); code mirroring strategies
